desc:
'Ever on, ever on,' they say of this road, which carries your weight
effortlessly upon its back through the hills and vales that populate this land.
You can almost hear the sea instead of the cobbles upon which you tread, and can
almost hear Cirdan calling you to join him and follow him across the sea. But
you shake away these dreams and see only hills around you.
name: Road to the Grey Havens

desc:
Veering around a particularly impassable hill, the road makes due by bending
along its length, like the slither of a snake. Even this mighty road must bow
down to the downs which surround it. The hills, like waves in an earthen sea,
smother the road and bind it like a prisoner, forcing it to run like water in
their cracks.
name: Road to the Grey Havens

desc:
The road makes a bend around a lesser hill with a single tree on its top and
thereby run east and south. To the east the road goes straight through some low
hills and the hillsides to your west blocks the way in that direction.
name: Road to the Grey Havens

desc:
Looking along this great road, you see a grassy flat begin to eat away at the
hills, stretching to your south, and emerging to your north, farther east. You
wonder if perhaps, the end of these hills is near, and if it is, what must lie
beyond? But then in the distance, as if answering your silent question, you see
more hills, as if the plains were a mere interlude in their parade.
name: Road to the Grey Havens

desc:
The road runs through the edge of the Shire, stretching westward through the far
downs to the sea beyond. Constructed by men and elves, it cuts a straight line
through a grassy prairie. A few flowers are scattered among the thick grass that
lines the road, their scent barely discernible. Large shapes move from time to
time through the prairie further off the road, but it is impossible to determine
what they might be from here.
name: Road to the Grey Havens

desc:
The road to the sea goes ever and on right through a small grassy prairie, while
steep hills stand like walls to its sides. The stones of the road carry the
marks of many travellers and uncountable carts. The air is full of the smell of
flowers which grow in the medium high grass.
name: Road to the Grey Havens

desc:
Looking along this great road, you see a grassy flat begin to eat away at the
hills, stretching to your north, and emerging to your south, farther west. You
wonder if perhaps, the end of these hills is near, and if it is, what must lie
beyond? But then in the distance, as if answering your silent question, you see
more hills, as if the plains were a mere interlude in their parade.
name: Road to the Grey Havens

desc:
The hills hammer this road, like iron out of a forge, to suit them, and here,
such has the road been hammered, forced to bend itself around a mountainous hill
which imposes itself upon you, almost regal, like the king of the downs. Even
the road must pay its dues to the king, and contorts itself into a bow. The
other hills rise up around, like courtiers in the king hill's court, and the
road, his jester.
name: Road to the Grey Havens

desc:
The road bores onward through these downs like a blind mole, following some
hidden logic that only it knows. You stand in a valley between two hills, on a
road which seems to travel endlessly forward, you wonder if you will ever see
the end of the road, or even of these downs, which tower everywhere about you,
trapping you, enclosing you.
name: Road to the Grey Havens

desc:
'Ever on, ever on,' they say of this road, which carries your weight
effortlessly upon its back through the hills and vales that populate this land.
You can almost hear the sea instead of the cobbles upon which you tread, and can
almost hear Cirdan calling you to join him and follow him across the sea. But
you shake away these dreams and see only hills around you.
name: Road to the Grey Havens

desc:
The gravel road runs east and west through the rolling hills of the White Downs.
The sides of the road are covered in closely cropped grass. Just to the south of
the road, a long scar has cut itself a way through the hills. It might be
possible to descend down into it, but climbing back up seems a lot harder.
name: Road to the Grey Havens

desc:
The road is made out of brown and grey cobblestone, and it runs in an east-west
direction. Sloping slightly, it ascends into the White Downs to the west. A
short distance to the east, the town of Michel Delving lies proudly, with
several hobbit dwellings and gardens. Just off the road to the north stands a
large tower.
name: Road to the Grey Havens

desc:
A large gravel road runs in an east-west direction here, being crossed by a few
smaller paths going north and south. Clustered around the roadside lies several
hobbit dwellings, like small dents in the grassland. Most of the hobbit-holes
have carefully tended gardens around them, with the scent of flowers filling the
air. The hobbit village continues to the east, while the large road ascends
slightly to the west, as it climbs the White Downs. A large, thorny hedge
follows the path leading south.
name: Michel Delving

desc:
The gravel road continues through a hobbit village. Several dwellings and houses
stands on both sides of the road. Most of them are built in stone, but some
others are carved out of the ground itself. The largest house is a whitewashed
stone-building with a carved oak door, standing just south of the road. In the
distance to the west, the White Downs rise up to meet the horizon.
name: Michel Delving

desc:
The gravel here is hard-packet and trampled. The road broadens a bit in the
west. There lies the village of Michel Delving, scattered with hobbit dwellings.
A small hobbit burrow lies just to the south, marking the outskirts of the
village. Bordering the north side of the road stands an orchard, surrounded by
green fields. To the east, the large road continues.
name: The East Road

desc:
The large road, made of hard-packed gravel, goes onward in an east-west
direction. To the west it starts a slight ascension towards the White Downs. The
village of Michel Delving can be seen resting there, at the foot of the downs.
The southern side of the road harbours a large green field.
name: The East Road

desc:
A large gravel road runs through the fields here. It bends here to avoid a small
hill, lying just to the east. Fields of strange plants cover the ground to the
south. The road continues to the north, and to the west in a slight incline.
name: A Bend in the Road

desc:
The large road turns both south and eastward here, toward a small hill that
shields whatever lies beyond. To the west a small gradient leads up to an
orchard, while thick grasses cover the land to the northern side of the road
extending out as far as the eye can see.
name: A Bend in the Road

desc:
The gravel road bends its way around a small hill that lies just south. It looks
like it would not be too hard for anyone to climb up and watch the surroundings.
To the north lie vast plains, covered in tall grasses and a few bushes. The road
goes onward to the east and west.
name: The East Road

desc:
The road is made of hard-packed gravel worn over the course of many years. The
road cuts through some grassy plains, while heading eastwards or to the west
around a small hill. Just to the south of the road lies a large farmyard, where
a wooden and a stone building surrounds it.
name: The East Road

desc:
Hard-packed and dirty, the gravel road runs east and west through the plain,
dividing it in separate fields. To the north the grass grows quite tall and
dense, while it becomes more of a desolate wasteland to the south. Standing out
against the south-west fields is a building made of stone, lying just off the
road.
name: The East Road

desc:
The gravel road runs through the landscape, going east and west from here.
Frequently used, it has become hard-packed and worn. The fields to the south of
the road are laid in waste, while the overgrown grasslands lie in stark contrast
on the northern side.
name: The East Road

desc:
You tread upon a gravelly pass which traverses the valleys between the downs.
You walk, crunching upon hard stones and crab grass, among the shadows of silent
hills, which lay like scales of a dragon long buried. Little noise besides your
own, reaches your ears, and only an oppressive silence greets you. The hills,
like watchmen, say nothing, but seem to glare at your every move.
name: Far Downs Pass

desc:
You tread upon a gravelly pass which traverses the valleys between the downs.
You walk, crunching upon hard stones and crab grass, among the shadows of silent
hills, which lay like scales of a dragon long buried. Little noise besides your
own, reaches your ears, and only an oppressive silence greets you. The hills,
like watchmen, say nothing, but seem to glare at your every move.
name: Far Downs Pass

desc:
You tread upon a gravelly pass which traverses the valleys between the downs.
You walk, crunching upon hard stones and crab grass, among the shadows of silent
hills, which lay like scales of a dragon long buried. Little noise besides your
own, reaches your ears, and only an oppressive silence greets you. The hills,
like watchmen, say nothing, but seem to glare at your every move.
name: Far Downs Pass

desc:
You tread upon a gravelly pass which traverses the valleys between the downs.
You walk, crunching upon hard stones and crab grass, among the shadows of silent
hills, which lay like scales of a dragon long buried. Little noise besides your
own, reaches your ears, and only an oppressive silence greets you. The hills,
like watchmen, say nothing, but seem to glare at your every move.
name: Far Downs Pass

desc:
You tread upon a gravelly pass which traverses the valleys between the downs.
You walk, crunching upon hard stones and crab grass, among the shadows of silent
hills, which lay like scales of a dragon long buried. Little noise besides your
own, reaches your ears, and only an oppressive silence greets you. The hills,
like watchmen, say nothing, but seem to glare at your every move.
name: Far Downs Pass

desc:
You are on the top of a very small hill. One single old oak-tree stands here,
its branches groping about the sky. The view is slightly better here than from
the road and far to the west one can imagine seeing the blue sea.
name: A Small Lonely Hill

desc:
You pad across flat, grassy, turf. Your boots leaving no impression upon the
ground beneath them. In the distance, the gnarled hills of the downs surround
you, but here, in this open expanse, the grasses reign, spreading green like a
carpet over the earth. Only the occasional stag, bolting across your path,
assures you that you are not alone. Coralled by the road and the hills, the
prairie is cut from its other half, and forced to submit to the will of the
hills, and the feet of man.
name: Prairie

desc:
You pad across flat, grassy, turf. Your boots leaving no impression upon the
ground beneath them. In the distance, the gnarled hills of the downs surround
you, but here, in this open expanse, the grasses reign, spreading green like a
carpet over the earth. Only the occasional stag, bolting across your path,
assures you that you are not alone. Like an impassable wall, the hills loom up,
blocking the advance of the prairie as it blocks your travel. You see no real
pass through the dark depths of the downs.
name: Prairie

desc:
Travellers across the flat, grassy, turf leaving no footprints upon the ground
beneath them. In the distance, the gnarled hills of the downs surround this open
expanse, the grasses reign, spreading green like a carpet over the earth. Only
the occasional stag, bolting across the landscape, reminds visitors that this
place is inhabited. Like an impassable wall, the hills loom up, blocking the
advance of the prairie as it blocks your travel. No real pass through the dark
depths of the downs can be seen.
name: Prairie

desc:
You pad across flat, grassy, turf. Your boots leaving no impression upon the
ground beneath them. In the distance, the gnarled hills of the downs surround
you, but here, in this open expanse, the grasses reign, spreading green like a
carpet over the earth. Only the occasional stag, bolting across your path,
assures you that you are not alone.
name: Prairie

desc:
You tread upon a gravelly pass which traverses the valleys between the downs.
You walk, crunching upon hard stones and crab grass, among the shadows of silent
hills, which lay like scales of a dragon long buried. Little noise besides your
own, reaches your ears, and only an oppressive silence greets you. The hills,
like watchmen, say nothing, but seem to glare at your every move.
name: Far Downs Pass

desc:
Upon a small hillock you stand. Its crown covered in a soft mane of grasses,
waving like hair in a gentle wind. From here you can see the downs spread out
before you, like massive sleeping giants, hibernating under a blanket of earth
and stone. You can even taste a slight salt in the air, as if the sea itself is
calling out to you, calling you to Cirdan for that final journey across the
seas.
name: Hillock

desc:
You pad across flat, grassy, turf. Your boots leave no impression upon the
ground beneath them. In the eastern distance, the White Downs rise like a wall
that ends the flat prairie, but here, in this open expanse, the grasses reign,
spreading green like a carpet over the earth. In the distance you can see dots
that may be deer or wild oxen, and not far to the east smoke rises from the
chimneys of a small village.
name: Prairie

desc:
Long, straight fences mark the boundary between this small farm and the prairie.
In place of the prairie's wildness, everything is neatly ordered in the farm.
Most of the land is used for growing crops, but there is a chicken coop by a
small rise. A small rise on one side of the farm boasts windows, a smoking
chimney, and a small round door.
name: Small Farm

desc:
You walk upon an old trail, almost lost, which travels, like a spectre, through
the downs around you. Passing through small valleys, losing itself in shadows,
you wonder if you will lose sight of it altogether, but, always, it reappears in
front of you, drawing you on.
name: Forgotten Trail

desc:
You walk upon an old trail, almost lost, which travels, like a specter, through
the downs around you. Passing through small valleys, losing itself in shadows,
you wonder if you will lose sight of it altogether, but, always, it reappears in
front of you, drawing you on.
name: Forgotten Trail

desc:
You walk upon an old trail, almost lost, which travels, like a specter, through
the downs around you. Passing through small valleys, losing itself in shadows,
you wonder if you will lose sight of it altogether, but, always, it reappears in
front of you, drawing you on.
name: Forgotten Trail

desc:
You walk upon an old trail, almost lost, which travels, like a spectre, through
the downs around you. Passing through small valleys, losing itself in shadows,
you wonder if you will lose sight of it altogether, but, always, it reappears in
front of you, drawing you on.
name: Forgotten Trail

desc:
You walk upon an old trail, almost lost, which travels, like a spectre, through
the downs around you. Passing through small valleys, losing itself in shadows,
you wonder if you will lose sight of it altogether, but, always, it reappears in
front of you, drawing you on.
name: Forgotten Trail

desc:
Tales say the Thinker's Hill got its name from the fact a strange hermit has
lived here seemingly forever. It is said he sits upon this hill and ponders the
times since men and elves first carved roads through these lonely downs. The
only structures which deface this hill or give it substance, are the Hermit's
shack and an old well.
name: Thinker's Hill

desc:
You're standing in a small ramshackle house. The rafters are held together with
bits of rotted twine and the floor is simply trampled earth. Pieces of coloured
cloth adorn the walls and a square rock in the centre of the floor seems to
serve as a table, or a bed, or maybe both. It smells quite foetid in here,
odours of sweat, decay, and perhaps even excrement assault your nose. You are
certainly glad that this home is not yours.
name: Hermit's Shack

desc:
You pad across flat, grassy, turf. Your boots leaving no impression upon the
ground beneath them. In the distance, the gnarled hills of the downs surround
you, but here, in this open expanse, the grasses reign, spreading green like a
carpet over the earth. Only the occasional stag, bolting across your path,
assures you that you are not alone. Like an impassable wall, the hills loom up,
blocking the advance of the prairie as it blocks your travel. You see no real
pass through the dark depths of the downs.
name: Prairie

desc:
You pad across flat, grassy, turf. Your boots leaving no impression upon the
ground beneath them. In the distance, the gnarled hills of the downs surround
you, but here, in this open expanse, the grasses reign, spreading green like a
carpet over the earth. Only the occasional stag, bolting across your path,
assures you that you are not alone. Like an impassable wall, the hills loom up,
blocking the advance of the prairie as it blocks your travel. You see no real
pass through the dark depths of the downs.
name: Prairie

desc:
This could well be the highest hilltop in the area, since visibility on the
surrounding hills and valleys is so good. Located just north of a large road it
also serves as excellent watching post for passing travellers.
name: Hilltop

desc:
You pad across flat, grassy, turf. Your boots leaving no impression upon the
ground beneath them. In the distance, the gnarled hills of the downs surround
you, but here, in this open expanse, the grasses reign, spreading green like a
carpet over the earth. Only the occasional stag, bolting across your path,
assures you that you are not alone. Coralled by the road and the hills, the
prairie is cut from its other half, and forced to submit to the will of the
hills, and the feet of man.
name: Prairie

desc:
You pad across flat, grassy, turf. Your boots leaving no impression upon the
ground beneath them. In the distance, the gnarled hills of the downs surround
you, but here, in this open expanse, the grasses reign, spreading green like a
carpet over the earth. Only the occasional stag, bolting across your path,
assures you that you are not alone.
name: Prairie

desc:
You pad across flat, grassy, turf. Your boots leaving no impression upon the
ground beneath them. In the distance, the gnarled hills of the downs surround
you, but here, in this open expanse, the grasses reign, spreading green like a
carpet over the earth. Only the occasional stag, bolting across your path,
assures you that you are not alone.
name: Prairie

desc:
You pad across flat, grassy, turf. Your boots leaving no impression upon the
ground beneath them. In the distance, the gnarled hills of the downs surround
you, but here, in this open expanse, the grasses reign, spreading green like a
carpet over the earth. Only the occasional stag, bolting across your path,
assures you that you are not alone.
name: Prairie

desc:
You pad across flat, grassy, turf. Your boots leaving no impression upon the
ground beneath them. In the distance, the gnarled hills of the downs surround
you, but here, in this open expanse, the grasses reign, spreading green like a
carpet over the earth. Only the occasional stag, bolting across your path,
assures you that you are not alone.
name: Prairie

desc:
You pad across flat, grassy, turf. Your boots leaving no impression upon the
ground beneath them. In the distance, the gnarled hills of the downs surround
you, but here, in this open expanse, the grasses reign, spreading green like a
carpet over the earth. Only the occasional stag, bolting across your path,
assures you that you are not alone.
name: Prairie

desc:
This wide prairie is covered in high grass. In place the grass becomes very thin
and bits brownish soil peek out. In the distance, the gnarled hills of the downs
look like they seem to surround this plane. Few animal tracks can be seen. Yet,
an occasional stag seems to pass or stop for a mouthful of the green grass from
time to time.
name: Prairie

desc:
The prairie grass in this section is quite thin and its colour is more yellow
then green. The soil seems to be quite dry and many small stones, most about the
size of a fist some much smaller, lie all around. In some spots, a cluster of
thistles have taken root in the earth.
name: Prairie

desc:
Thin but high grass covers this prairie. While many higher hills can be seen in
the distance, many smaller ones cover the plain. Over the time a population of
moles seems to have done a good deal of digging and have left their low heaps of
soft earth all around. Apart from the moles, many other animals of all sizes
have left their marks by nibbling on or walking through the grass.
name: Prairie

desc:
The prairie stretches on in all directions, while low grass forms a dense carpet
in the sections between the occasional low bushes. The bushes are sparse, their
branches are brown and the leaves a very thin green.
name: Prairie

desc:
You pad across flat, grassy, turf. Your boots leaving no impression upon the
ground beneath them. In the distance, the gnarled hills of the downs surround
you, but here, in this open expanse, the grasses reign, spreading green like a
carpet over the earth. Only the occasional stag, bolting across your path,
assures you that you are not alone.
name: Prairie

desc:
You pad across flat, grassy, turf. Your boots leaving no impression upon the
ground beneath them. In the distance, the gnarled hills of the downs surround
you, but here, in this open expanse, the grasses reign, spreading green like a
carpet over the earth. Only the occasional stag, bolting across your path,
assures you that you are not alone. Like an impassable wall, the hills loom up,
blocking the advance of the prairie as it blocks your travel. You see no real
pass through the dark depths of the downs.
name: Prairie

desc:
You pad across flat, grassy, turf. Your boots leaving no impression upon the
ground beneath them. In the distance, the gnarled hills of the downs surround
you, but here, in this open expanse, the grasses reign, spreading green like a
carpet over the earth. Only the occasional stag, bolting across your path,
assures you that you are not alone. Here the hills close around the prairie like
a fist, barring your movement to only the open ground. The hills seem ominous,
and you wonder, in their dark depths, just what secrets they are hiding.
name: Prairie

desc:
You pad across flat, grassy, turf. Your boots leaving no impression upon the
ground beneath them. In the distance, the gnarled hills of the downs surround
you, but here, in this open expanse, the grasses reign, spreading green like a
carpet over the earth. Only the occasional stag, bolting across your path,
assures you that you are not alone. Coralled by the road and the hills, the
prairie is cut from its other half, and forced to submit to the will of the
hills, and the feet of man.
name: Prairie

desc:
You see row upon row of clay-made coffins, sarcophagus upon sarcophagus, lining
the walls up to the ceiling. Yet, as you look closer, you notice the coffins
have been vandalized. They are destroyed and broken-up in many places and
strange marks have scratched out the runes which once covered the coffins and
the walls. The smell is oppressively musty here, and you feel your pulse race as
you begin to scan the room more and more quickly.
name: Temple Crypt

desc:
This small cave looks like it was once the burial chamber of some priest. The
walls, seemingly once lavishly decorated with symbols, are now just a collection
of scratches. The large sarcophagus has been savagely broken into and thrown
against the southern wall, while all the once beautiful pottery which once
graced the floor has been destroyed. The entire southern wall has been knocked
down from the outside, the walls being bent in and crumbled, and not able to
save this chamber from being molested. A broken sarcophagus can be seen along
that wall.
name: Burial Crypt

desc:
This place might once have been a bunk room, but now is simply a mess. Nothing
is in order. Things are simply strewn everywhere as if some chaotic whirlwind
had blown through. A lack of dust upon anything might suggest that this happened
quite recently. What reason someone may have had in tossing around this grouping
of worthless junk is a mystery.
name: Lair of the Ratheads

desc:
This is small but surprisingly clean alcove, totally empty but for a pile of
decaying coffins which line the western wall. A quick glance around reveals that
this room has been abandoned for a long time. Dust covers all but the most
recent tracks of small rodents. The walls are smooth, devoid of any design or
fault, making one ponder what use this room might have once had.
name: Alcove

desc:
Hollowed out by water, this cave is the shape of a huge overturned bowl, its
ceiling lost in shadows. Water gathers in a dark pool in its centre, perhaps the
remnants of an underground river, now, merely a dark pond. With every
traveller's step the clatter of equipment echos wildly through the cave. A dark
stream is here, trickling from under the western wall, terminating in a small
pond.
name: Cave Hollow

desc:
Whether it was carved out by the hands of men, or whether it was hollowed by
some force of nature you know not. All you see is the bowl-shaped stone walls of
the pit which seem to oppress you. The dripping of water from the rock is a
torture to your hearing and the musty smell causes you to cough despite
yourself. Was this where you were destined to end your travels?
name: Dark Pit

desc:
You find yourself on a soft earth trail running through a section of the white
downs. So called for the chalk that covers them, they rise up to greet you like
so many bleached skulls, as if they once all had faces which have eroded with
time. You make little noise as you move through this graveyard of hills, but you
always imagine you hear something, or see something, out of the corner of your
eye, which always disappears.
name: Chalk Hills Trail

desc:
You find yourself on a soft earth trail running through a section of the white
downs. So called for the chalk that covers them, they rise up to greet you like
so many bleached skulls, as if they once all had faces which have eroded with
time. You make little noise as you move through this graveyard of hills, but you
always imagine you hear something, or see something, out of the corner of your
eye, which always disappears.
name: Chalk Hills Trail

desc:
You find yourself on a soft earth trail running through a section of the white
downs. So called for the chalk that covers them, they rise up to greet you like
so many bleached skulls, as if they once all had faces which have eroded with
time. You make little noise as you move through this graveyard of hills, but you
always imagine you hear something, or see something, out of the corner of your
eye, which always disappears.
name: Chalk Hills Trail

desc:
You climb up the rough slope and finally find yourself standing upon the King
Hill. Standing tallest among the hills, it reigns regally over the downs that
surround it. Cool winds sail through you, chilling your bones and causing you to
involuntarily shiver. A green grass covers its summit, a living crown upon the
King Hill. Though grass covers its top, its sides are covered in a skin of
chalk, an immovable face, weathered and bleached by the ages. Looking about from
this vantage point, you imagine seeing all of the lands bending out before you,
and here, looking down at them, you imagine ruling it all, and you understand
why they call it the King Hill.
name: The King Hill

desc:
You are standing in a small building of stone, which sits, like a watchtower
upon this hill. There are windows on all sides, and a small bed in the corner.
All that graces the rest of the room is a simple table. Though it looks
deserted, everything is remarkably clean and dust-free, like it is still,
perhaps, lived in. You feel at home here, at peace.
name: Ranger Outpost

desc:
You stand upon a hill so named for a small ranger outpost that graces its
summit. Though the sides of the hill are covered in a skin of chalk, the path
you took up its side was smoothly sloped and covered with grass. The outpost is
small, but reasonably well-kept, and you can enter it by travelling farther up
the hill. Looking out, you can see across the downs, and, looking west, you even
think you see the spiralling towers of the Grey Havens.
name: Ranger's Hill

desc:
This massive dry trench has sunk well below the level of the surrounding hills.
The sides of the gorge are steep, and white from exposed rocks strewn about by
frequent small rockslides. The ground towards the northern end of the scar is
rubble-strewn, and the slope to the north looks impossible to ascend.
name: A Gorge

desc:
The gorge runs in a north-south direction, like a scar in the surrounding
countryside. Finding itself more than sixty feet below the surrounding hills,
not much sunlight ever reaches the ground. Even so, there are a few stunted
weeds growing in between the boulders that cover the dry valley-floor. A few
broken animal bones lie scattered at places.
name: A Gorge

desc:
The narrow gorge turns eastward through the downs. The steep cliff-sides prevent
any travel further to the south and west. In several places the cliff has
collapsed, leaving shallow scree slopes. Large rocks and a great deal of rubble
lie scattered all over the ground.
name: A Gorge

desc:
The ground of this area is dry and rubble-strewn, from frequent small
rock-falls. It is a narrow cleft, about sixty feet below the level of the
surrounding hills. Carving its way through the White Downs, the gorge continues
to the west, while a scree slope leads to the east.
name: A Gorge

desc:
This old dirt path does not look to be used too often. The thorny hedge follows
the path as it bends towards the east or north, too high to make out anything
lying behind it. A scree slope leads down into a gorge, marring the White Downs
to the west.
name: The Path to Sarn Ford

desc:
You drop down into a large hole dug underneath an almost solid floor of garbage.
Several roughly hewn shelves hold the things the Ratheads hold dear...junk. The
cave itself is very musty and damp, water, and other liquids dripping from the
garbage above the trap door. The smell of what you can only hope is excrement
fills your nostrils, sending immense images of squalor and filth to your mind's
eye.
name: Rathead Storeroom

desc:
Green and healthy apple trees stand all around this grass-covered orchard. Just
to the south of the orchard runs a large gravel road, while a smaller dirt path
is seen to the west. There is a large sign next to the orchard, with a
hand-written notice upon it.
name: An Orchard

desc:
Closely cropped grass covers this field. It looks like a grazing land for
cattle. Just to the west of the pasture there is a path running alongside the
White Downs. The fields continue to the east and north, while several apple
trees forming an orchard stand to the south.
name: A Cow Pasture

desc:
This field is closely cropped grassland. It is most likely used as a pasture for
cattle, judging by the amount of cow dung lying everywhere. To the north lies a
small, wooden farmhouse. The grazing lands extend for miles to the south and
east. To the west a dirty path runs in a north-south direction.
name: A Cow Pasture

desc:
A large oaken table and the chairs arranged around it, dominate this small
farmhouse. The floor is made out of several wooden planks. It is covered with
dirt. Two small, round windows sit in the eastern wall, providing a view of the
pasture lying there. Resting against the western wall are a couple of bunk-beds.
A door leading out of the house is situated in the south wall.
name: A Small Farmhouse

desc:
A small dirt-path runs at the base of the White Downs, lying protectively to the
west. To the east of here lies a pasture of lush grass, and a small farmhouse
can be seen to the north-east. The dirt-path continues up a hill to the north,
and towards the road, several miles to the south. A few large footprints can be
seen on the path.
name: A Path Leading North

desc:
Following the eastern edge of the White Downs, this dirt-path runs in a
north-south direction, slowly ascending towards a hill in the north. Just to the
east of the path, green pastures stretch lazily. A larger gravel road can be
seen a few miles to the south.
name: A Path Leading North

desc:
This path goes northwards into the White Downs, following their eastern edge. To
the west, the downs create leeward, sheltering the path from any wind. However,
the abruptness of the cliffs also makes it impossible to travel in that
direction. Just to the east of the path, an orchard can be seen, while a large
road runs past to the south.
name: A Path Leading North

desc:
Cattle-hooves have churned the soil here, making it muddy. A few tufts of grass
grow at uneven intervals. Imprinted in the mud are tracks of small animals, as
well as those of cattle. Just to the west of the pasture stands a pleasant
little farmhouse. Grassland of tall grasses lies to the east, while knee-high
grass cover the pasturage to the south.
name: A Cow Pasture

desc:
This fallow field is covered in knee-high grasses and weeds. The pasture has not
been ploughed for a few years. Grazing lands, looking better tended, lie to the
north and west of here. To the east grassland stretches on for miles.
name: A Fallow Pasture

desc:
Knee-high grasses and weeds grow wildly covering this field. The soil beneath
the unkempt grass is hard and having not been worked on for some time. There are
similar looking lands to the north of here, whilst the pasture to the west look
more recently tended. For several miles to the east tall grasses can be seen.
Just to the south of this field, an orchard sits against the road.
name: A Fallow Pasture

desc:
Several apple trees stand in this grass-covered orchard. A few rotting
windfall-apples litter the ground below the trees. A thorny hedge has been
planted in the south border of the orchard, shielding it from the road that runs
there. The orchard continues a couple of miles to the west. To the north lies a
fallow field.
name: An Orchard

desc:
Thick, tall grass grows here, providing food and shelter for the animals in the
area. The soil is a bit muddy, and tracks of many small creatures can be seen.
The grassy lands extend far to the east and south. A pasture for grazing animals
can be seen to the west.
name: Grassland

desc:
Tall and thick grass covers the dry ground, rolling like waves of a green sea.
Trampled straws form a narrow trail, leading to a pond in the east. The
grasslands extend for miles to the south and west. Against the horizon in the
west, the white downs can be seen.
name: Grassland

desc:
A large, shallow pond rests here. Its muddy perimeter is filled with animal
tracks, as well as some growing rush dipping in the water. The water in the
middle of the pond looks a bit cleaner, and can be reached by wading a few
yards. Wildly growing shrubbery and thistle can be seen in the grasslands to the
east. Less overgrown grasslands stretch far to the west and south.
name: A Pond

desc:
The grey-green grass that grows here sways lazily in the breeze. A few bushes
and patches of clover also dot the plain here and there. The soil is dark and
somewhat muddy, filled with prints from various animals. Just to the north lies
a large, shallow pond. In every other direction, the grasslands continue,
somewhat overgrown in the east.
name: Grassland

desc:
Tall grasses, grey-green in colour, cover the dark soil. A few bushes stand
against a gravel road passing by in the south. Similar fields of grass lie to
the north and west, while overgrown grasslands covered in shrubbery and thistle
lie to the east.
name: Grassland

desc:
Thick, green grasses and a few bushes dot the land here. The soil is made of
dark earth, and it is quite dry. Fields of grasses stretch for miles in all
directions. South of here a large gravel road runs through the grassland. A
small hill rises beyond it.
name: Grassland

desc:
Vast grasslands extend for miles to the north and east. The grasses are thick
and tall, in a greyish shade of green. The soil is somewhat muddy, but still
dry. A fallow field stretches to the west. Just to the south of here a large
road passes by.
name: Grassland

desc:
The ground is covered in gently swaying, tall grass. A few, large patches of
clover break the monotony of the rolling grassland. These plains stretch further
in all directions, except to the west, where a fallow pasture lies.
name: Grassland

desc:
Tall grass, swaying gently, grows in abundance upon these plains. Only a few
patches of clover at scattered intervals break the monotone landscape. The
grasslands stretch forever on, in all directions. Beyond them to the west, white
downs rise against the skyline.
name: Grassland

desc:
The land here is reverted to scrub, and covered in wildly growing shrubbery.
Rough grasses grow beneath the bushes and a few patches of clover and thistle
mar the landscape. Faint animal trails lead to the west, where a large, shallow
pond resides. In all other directions, the grassland continues its wild growth.
name: Overgrown Grassland

desc:
High stems of thistle reach up towards the sky, as well as overgrown scrub and
bushes dotting the land everywhere. A few small trees and young saplings are
also present among the bushes. Some animals have made faintly outlined trails on
the ground. For miles to both the south and the west, these overgrown grasslands
continue.
name: Overgrown Grassland

desc:
Stretching for miles in every direction, rough grasses extend among bushes of
various sizes. There are stems of thistle, reaching high towards the sky,
growing everywhere. Patches of clover also dot the grassland at several
occasions.
name: Overgrown Grassland

desc:
This plain is overgrown by thicket and high-stemmed thistles. Patches of clover
and tufts of rough grasses also dot the landscape. These overgrown grasslands
continue for miles in all directions, but they look somewhat less covered with
scrub to the west. In the northwest a muddy pond can be seen.
name: Overgrown Grassland

desc:
Rough, tall grasses grow densely upon the dry ground. Several high-stemmed
thistles and a few thorny thickets also grow scattered around the grassland. The
overgrown fields continue for miles to the north and to the east. In the south
the scrub gives way to a large gravel road running by in an east-west direction.
name: Overgrown Grassland

desc:
The rough grass here grows high and wild, looking to have been neither tended
nor cropped in a while. Blotting the grasslands at several places are high stems
of thistle, and a few small bushes. The overgrown lands continue to the north
and west, while the East Road runs by just to the south.
name: Overgrown Grassland

desc:
White walls surround this large room. Several portraits of well-fed hobbits,
possibly past Shire Mayors, hang on the walls. A desk stands near the entrance.
It is strewn with yellowing papers and scrolls. Two large windows are set in the
western wall of this building, while a large cupboard covers the southern end of
the room.
name: The Mayor's Office

desc:
This is a small and dingy room. A thick layer of dust has settled on the floor.
Several shelves are attached to the south wall, holding thousands of old papers
and ledgers. This room looks empty and is probably not used too often.
name: A Cupboard

desc:
This faint trail turns towards the south-east. The lands surrounding the trail
seem to be desolate and dry. To the east, the path is even more faint and dusty.
The White Downs obstruct any attempt at travelling to the west and south.
name: A Bend in the Path

desc:
The path here is very faint. It leads both to the west, below the towering White
Downs, and the south among desolate fields. There is a hedge lining the northern
and eastern sides of the trail. The area to the north looks to be covered in
extremely dense vegetation. A hole can be spotted in the eastern hedge,
providing a view of an old cottage.
name: The Path to Sarn Ford

desc:
The faint path is overgrown by thorny bushes here, preventing any travel onwards
to the south and east. It does not look like anyone has used this trail for a
very long time. The White Downs can be seen some distance to the west.
name: The Path to Sarn Ford

desc:
The dirt path continues its course towards the south-east. It does not seem to
be used much. The back of an old, wooden building stands to the north. The
surrounding area is very dry and barren, save a few bushes growing around the
path, covering it at some places. The silhouette of a tall tree can be seen
beyond the wastelands to the east.
name: A Bend in the Path

desc:
An old well stands outside an old, ruined cottage. However, it is filled with
mud instead of water. The door of the building to the south seems solid enough,
but the roof could collapse at any moment. A large, thorny hedge grows to the
west. There is a hole in the hedge, revealing a path on the other side. To the
north lies a field of hay, while a ploughed field lies just to the east of here.
name: Outside an Old, Ruined Cottage

desc:
The scent of hay fills the air, emanating from a few heaps of hay that lie on
the ground and a few haystacks standing here and there. Several dense bushes
grow behind a hedge to the west, preventing any movement in that direction. To
the south of the field the remains of a rather decayed old building stand.
Fields of grasses and other plants are located to the north and east of here.
name: A Field of Hay

desc:
Lush, green grasses sway lazily in the wind here. The vegetation grows denser to
the west, preventing travel in that direction. A hobbit burrow can be seen to
the north-west, marking the edge of the Michel Delving village. To the north
lies the large gravel road called by many The East Road. Irregular patches of
small fields lie to the south and east. They seem to be well cared for.
name: A Green Field

desc:
The dank smell of decay hangs heavily in the air here. Several large holes in
the roof have clearly been allowing in the rain for some considerable time. The
tattered remains of some furniture lie scattered around the cottage. This ruined
cottage has clearly been abandoned for quite some time.
name: A Ruined Cottage

desc:
The barren ground here consists of dry dirt. There are a few thistles growing
here and there, but otherwise this is a wasteland. Beyond the continuing
wastelands to the east, a tall tree can be spotted. The fields to the north have
been ploughed recently, but nothing grows there yet. A faint, dusty path lies to
the west.
name: Wasteland

desc:
Deep furrows have been ploughed into this field recently. It does not look like
anything has been sowed here yet, and the ground is quite muddy. To the north
and east, large grain-fields can be seen. The lands in the south however, are
barren and waste. Just to the west of this field stands and old, ruined cottage.
name: A Ploughed Field

desc:
Rich and fertile soil covers the ground of this field. Stout stems of grain grow
upon it, supporting large heads of wheat. These fields continue for miles to the
east, while the ploughed fields to the south do not seem to be sowed yet. In the
north, patches of some strange plant covers the ground. A ruined cottage can be
seen to the south-west, standing next to a field of hay.
name: A Field

desc:
The earth is dark and rich here, seemingly well tended. Growing about this field
are patches of some strange root crop. Concealed by the dark soil are brown and
wrinkled tubers. To the south the root crop gives way to a grain-field. The land
grows more lush and green to the west of these fields. An apple orchard stands
to the east, while a large gravel road runs past in the north.
name: A Field

desc:
This small hilltop rises above the fields that stretch in all directions. Rich
pastures and ploughed fields lie far away to the northwest and some in the south
as well. A large gravel road turns around the hill west and north. The
grasslands in the northeast look overgrown and wild, while barren wasteland
stretch for miles far in the southeast. At the slopes of the hill, just to the
east lies a small farm, while beyond the patchwork of orchards and hay-fields to
the south a single, tall tree stands against the skyline.
name: A Small Hill

desc:
A dozen old and gnarled apple trees stand here. Some of them have broken boughs
from bearing too much fruit. To the south of the orchard lies a field of wheat,
while several haystacks can be seen to the east. There is a field lying to the
west as well, but it is hard to discern what is growing upon it.
name: An Apple Orchard

desc:
The earth here seems very fertile, and stems of wheat-grains grow everywhere.
The fields of wheat continue for a few miles both to the west and east. Some
other plants growing in the field to the south. An orchard with several large
apple-trees borders this area in the north. In the distance to the south-east,
the shadow of a very tall, lone tree can be seen.
name: A Field

desc:
The dark soil has recently been worked. Some odd plants with large green leaves
cover this field. Large fields of tall grains lie in the north. To the west, a
recently ploughed field rests, while the fields to the east and south looks
unused for some time. Reaching towards the sky in the south-east stands a large
solitary tree.
name: A Field

desc:
The soil here is dark and dry. What once might have been flourishing fields, now
lie infertile and desolate. The wasteland goes on into the west. The lands to
the east look barren as well, but for a single, dark and tall tree looming
overhead. To the north, some plants grow upon a recently worked field.
name: Wasteland

desc:
This dirt-covered farmyard rests at the slopes of a small hill, which rises in
the west. A small crude plough sits next the walls of the wooden barn in the
south, as well as a broken old cart that lies in the middle of the farmyard. To
the east sits a larger stone building, possibly the home of the farmer, its
window shutters being closed to prevent anyone from peering inside. Just to the
north of the farmyard, the East Road runs its course.
name: A Farmyard

desc:
The scent of hay fills the air. Several golden-hued ricks are rising at
intervals upon this field, their contents being tinder-dry grass. A few trees
grow around the field as well. In the north lies a wooden building next to a
small hill, while desolate wastelands lie in the east. Slightly cultivated
fields lie to the south and south-west, while an apple orchard displays itself
to the west.
name: A Hay Field

desc:
Large and stout stalks of grain grow upon this fertile and recently worked
field. The grain supports large heads of wheat. The fields of wheat continue
into the west. Several haystacks can be seen in a hay-field to the north. To the
south and east the fields give way to a more desolate landscape. Beyond the
wasteland to the south, the form of a large and lonely tree can be seen.
name: A Field

desc:
Dry soil and dirt cover the barren ground. Scrub and a few thistles grow here
and there, dotting the landscape. While the few rough grasses have all been
trampled into the soil by some animal. To the south stands a rather tall tree,
its leaves rustling gently. To the west and north slightly more cultivated
fields can be seen, while the infertile wasteland continues to the east.
name: Wasteland

desc:
A large, lonely tree stands in the middle of the otherwise barren wasteland. Its
strong boughs look able to support a lot of weight. Climbing up there would
possibly offer a revealing view about the surrounding countryside. The ground
around the tree is dry and brown, and covered with a few fallen leaves. In all
directions, the wastelands continue for miles.
name: An Isolated Tree

desc:
Scrub-covered wasteland stretches for miles in all direction. The few bushes
growing nearby are scrubby and crude. The rough grass is trampled into the soil
by some medium-sized animal. To the west stands a single, tall and dark tree.
The wasteland covers all the other directions, however there are some stone
structure situated in the wasteland to the east.
name: Wasteland

desc:
For miles in every direction, lie barren fields of dirt and thistle. The brown
soil is poor and infertile, but still a few saplings grow here and there,
clinging to the ground. Rough grass has been trampled into the dirt by animals.
To the south-west a tall and lonely tree reaches high into the sky.
name: Wasteland

desc:
Brownish, dry dirt covers these fields. Only a few bushes and saplings dot the
landscape at a couple of places, otherwise the ground is desolate. The infertile
wastes stretch in all directions, but it looks like the fields to the west are a
bit more cared for.
name: Wasteland

desc:
The barren fields of rough dirt and unfriendly scrub extend for miles in all
directions, except the west, where fields of hay can be seen. The ground here is
covered in dark, dry soil that has not been worked on for quite some time. A few
lone bushes and a couple of high-stemmed thistles grow sporadically.
name: Wasteland

desc:
This land, not being worked for years, has reverted into scrub and barren
wasteland. The soil has become infertile and dark. A few, trampled grasses grow
among the scrub. To the west lies a stone building, while a large gravel road
can be spotted in the north. The wastes continue for miles in the other
directions.
name: Wasteland

desc:
This tidy dwelling is filled with well-polished, sturdy furniture. The flagstone
floor is very clean and obviously swept frequently. Against the north-wall rests
a grate, with a fire crackling. Above the fire hangs a large pot, its contents
bubbling slowly.
name: A Stone Farmhouse

desc:
This wooden building has a very low ceiling. The planks used to build this barn
are thick and dry. Stacked against the southern wall of the building are several
sacks of grain. At least one of the sacks has been torn allowing hundreds of
grains to litter the floor of the barn. A few broken crates create a bigger mess
below some shelves in the western part of the room.
name: A Wooden Barn

desc:
The brownish soil of this wasteland looks infertile and hard. The land has been
reverted to scrub, with a few thistles growing sporadically. Animals have
trampled the grass everywhere, but a few saplings can still be discerned here
and there. To the north lies a large road, which travels in an east-west
direction. The wasteland stretches to the south and west of here, but a patch of
green disrupt the infertile harmony just to the south.
name: Wasteland

desc:
This shallow depression stands out in the surrounding wasteland. Ruins of an old
cottage lie here. Although, virtually nothing remains of the old wrecked
building, the garden is still almost intact. It is covered with bushes and a few
trees, as well as overgrown beds of flower.
name: An Overgrown Garden

desc:
The brown dirt has not been worked for years, making the soil hard and
infertile. A few shrubs still grow here though, marking the otherwise bare
wasteland. A few patches of short grass growing about have been trampled and
eaten to ground level. The wasteland continues in all directions, but a patch of
green can be seen to the north. A bird pond lies to the east.
name: Wasteland

desc:
Rough dirt and soil covers the ground in every direction. Small scrub and
thistle grows all around, dotting the landscape. The few rough grasses have all
been trampled into the soil by some animal. The wasteland continues in all
directions, but an odd stone structure can be seen to the south. In the east
lies a large pond.
name: Wasteland

desc:
A provisional stone pen has been built here, but it looks long abandoned. The
floor of the pen is strewn with weathered cattle-bones. To the east and south,
crumbling stone walls offer some protection against the wind. The wasteland
surrounding the pen continues for miles to the north and west.
name: A Stone Pen

desc:
The foliage here is moving gently in the wind. From up in this tree one can see
for miles in all directions. A narrow line through a patchwork of fields to the
north marks a large road. To the west, a faint path runs alongside the White
Downs stretching for miles to the north. Barren wastelands cover the land to the
east and south.
name: Up in a Tree

desc:
This dark and musty tunnel seems to be used as a warehouse. There are bundles of
coarse material and large wooden crates littering the floor. There are some
barrels piled up against the eastern wall and stacks of boxes seem to be
covering something to the north.
name: Storage Tunnel

desc:
This tunnel seems very old. The floor is covered with debris from the crumbling
ceiling as well as in a thick layer of rubble. The walls of the tunnel are
infested with mould, which thrives in the damp and humid tunnels. Strangely
enough a footpath has been cleared through the rubble, as if this tunnel is
often in use.
name: The Tunnels

desc:
This tunnel is a narrow passage between the rubble-filled tunnel in the north
and a small cavern in the south. The rock-walls are range from pale yellow to
dark grey in colour, at places covered with sickly, grey-green mould.
name: A Narrow Tunnel

desc:
This small chamber has been deliberately carved out of the soft yellow rock. The
ground of the cavern is made of cracked stone, and there is a large flat stone
resting in the middle of it. Several alcoves have been cut into the sides of the
chamber. A few reminders of the previous occupants lie scattered about the
floor.
name: A Small Cavern

desc:
The extent of this large underground chamber may only be determined by
painstakingly following the wall all the way around. At several places,
stalactites have merged with stalagmites, forming large pillars. The entrance of
this cavern lies to the west, where the cavern ceiling is much lower.
name: A Cavern

desc:
Carved by some long vanished, underground river, the roof of this cave is quite
low. Small stalactites hang like daggers from the ceiling. In the middle of the
cave, some of the stalactites have been broken off, to open the cave for
exploration. The cave slopes gently upwards to the west, where large rocks rest
unnaturally at the cave-entrance. The grotto becomes much wider, and somewhat
higher to the east.
name: A Cave

desc:
In this room is a large feather bed with posts at each corner, each post carved
obviously by an artisan of great skill. The floor is a mosaic of an intricately
pieced, many coloured wood. Murals are painted on two of the walls, and there
are windows on the others. As you look up there is a window on the ceiling, that
lights up this room during the day, and lets moonlight in during the night.
name: A Cozy Bedroom

desc:
This comfortable hole protects the hobbits from the cold winds that blow over
the prairie during a Westfarthing winter. A long tunnel links the kitchen with
the bedrooms, larder, and dining room.
name: Hobbit Hole

desc:
This is a typical dwarven home cut into one of the hills. The floor is expensive
marble polished to a mirror-like finish. The walls have paintings and tapestries
depicting dwarven heroes. There is a tunnel continuing to the south from which
emanates a soft light.
name: Foyer

desc:
In the dim lighting of this large room a huge collection of objects can be seen,
amongst the shadows. On the southern and western walls there are several shelves
upon which the smaller mathoms are placed. The larger mathoms rest on a sturdy
oak stand against the eastern wall. A stack of dusty, cobweb-covered paintings
lean against the wall in one corner. There are also many books, some of which
lie open, as if someone read them not so long ago. To the south a few steps lead
up to a door.
name: The Mathom-house

desc:
The maker of this home took advantage of a crystal formation in the hill. During
the day sunlight filters down and creates a myriad of rainbows which shimmer in
the mist from a spring. The spring pours into a small basin in the floor before
it flows out again through a hole in the wall.
name: Crystal Room

desc:
You are standing in the bottom of a deep well. The sides are slick with
moisture, making its walls unclimbable. It is quite large in circumference, but
quite empty, the water it once held long gone. The ground squishes underneath
your boot and the air is quite chill, a draft blowing from some unknown source.
Your breath materialises in front of you, like a spirit rising from the
netherworld.
name: Bottom of the Well

desc:
In this room, the tree's bark runs from bottom to top. Small baskets of flowers
and plants hang from the branches which spread through the ceiling of this large
natural hallway. Seats made of wood, with pillows made of leaves are placed at
each corner of the room, serving as resting spots for the folk that pass by
here.
name: The Treehouse Hallway

desc:
A rack of weapons stands tall on the Eastern side of this room. From time to
time, swords, axes, spears, daggers, and myriad other types of weapons are
stored here. On the Western side of the room, leather, metal, and various
assortments of personal protection are hung against the wall. Use of this
equipment is reserved for the frequent practice and training battles which occur
here.
name: The Training Yard

desc:
Cracked walls, shaded windows and an old and noisy door compose this room. This
seems to be the bedroom of a great dwarven warrior, weapons hanging on the
walls, shields and pieces of armour over the chair. On the walls there are some
pictures of the fallen people of Arda. In the most comfortable corner there is a
sturdy bed where great dreams about glory and victory are made every night. By
its side, there is a little table full of beard-combs, mauve petals and wooden
pipes.
name: Gaulgon's Headquarters

desc:
This is apparenly the underside of a great hill. The room is lit up a bit by
some light that has filtered through the sides of the hatch above. The
surrounding walls are made of damp brown dirt and the quarters are quite cramped
with not much room for movement. Looking down its possible to see the dark
depths of the hill.
name: Underneath a Hatch

desc:
This is a long, dark, narrow tunnel leading even further down into the hill. The
walls are made of the same dirt as was seen underneath the hatch now far above.
Other than a few parts of the wall still lit up by the light coming from above,
the surroundings are difficult to see. However, further down there is a sense of
life coming from the chamber below, though from this vantage point it is still
shrouded in darkness.
name: A Long Passage Down

desc:
After travelling down from the damp tight tunnel above, there is finally enough
room for a full-grown person to stretch out their limbs. Looking back up through
the black tunnel, all that can be seen now is what looks like a tiny hatch far
above with light creeping through the sides. This is apparently very core of the
bottom of a great hill. The room is lit by a set of dim magical lights floating
around the room. From afar, the lights look like small faeries, but they seem to
avoid closer examination by floating away if anything draws near. The walls are
made of a silver metal accented with engravings of great armour and weapons
carved into the sides. This chamber clearly is the home of a great dwarf, who
might not appreciate the intrusion.
name: Nagoroth's Mighty Vault

desc:
This bedchamber is most inviting after a long adventure. There is a big bed,
which looks quite comfortable. There are pegs and shelves about the room to
store weapons and equipment.
name: Glimli's Private Room

desc:
You are in a big round room with green walls and high ceiling. Some windows are
situated around the room, letting in light during the daytime. Some strange
magical symbols are drawn on the walls around the door. The only furniture here
is a sturdy coat hanger by the door, and some chairs near the walls. A sturdy
spiral staircase leads upwards in the tower.
name: Anton's Green Tower

desc:
You are inside the round tower with green stone walls. Four large windows are
here, each one facing one of the four cardinal points, making it possible to
view the surroundings in every direction. Around the walls there are several
pictures on display. In the centre of the room, some comfortable armchairs and a
large oaken table stand. It is obvious that every effort has been made to make
this room as comfortable and relaxing as possible. A spiral staircase continues
both up and downwards in the tower.
name: Inside the Green Tower

desc:
You are at the highest point in the tower. It is a large room with a high
ceiling and the walls and floor is made out of stone. A lot of windows are
situated all around the walls, presenting a beautiful view and letting a lot of
light and fresh air into the room. A large oaken bed stands here, facing east,
and some chairs are placed in different locations around the room. It seems you
can rest here for ages without any disturbances and noise. A spiral staircase
wind its way downward in the tower.
name: Top of Anton's Green Tower

desc:
This is the entrance of a huge cavern. The entrance doesn't seem to be big
enough to allow many people inside. You see another room to the east that
instills a sense of great danger about this place. A wise person might think it
would be a good idea to leave quickly. On the ground you see footprints, tracked
in blood.
name: Cavern Entrance

desc:
This room evokes a feeling of strength. Seems it was made just for the purpose
of sitting and thinking about great fights and long travels. On the walls you
see pictures of great fights, and they seem to be the exploits of just one
person. When you look up you see large words made with fresh blood, 'KAO,' and
many scalps of Sauron's minions on the floor.
name: The Stone Foyer

desc:
At the top of the stairs, here is Kao's lair. You see some great pieces of
armour on the walls, and many different foods and drinks on the table. However,
there is just one glass... It seems the owner doesn't have many visitors, or
perhaps they are simply not welcome.
name: Kao's Lair

desc:
A small hill presents itself to the north so small that it looks like a parody
of some of the mightier hills further to the north. What forces of nature made
one hill small and another so grand is hard to guess. Surely each hill's role
was decided before the dawn of men and will remain long after the time of the
mortals living today has passed.
name: Road to the Grey Havens

desc:
This is a very short tunnel, only about fifty feet long from end to end. A few
torches line the way, throwing flickering light upon the walls which are overed
in scrawls and pictures. To the south, the passage ends in darkness, and an old
rickety door terminates it to the west.
name: Short Tunnel

desc:
The path continues its north-south way between the hedges here. The hedge
prevents any movement to the east, as well as blocking the view in that
direction. The White Downs rise in the west. It does not seem to be any
accessible climb up to them anywhere.
name: The Path to Sarn Ford

desc:
A faint path, lined by very high and thorny hedges, goes in a north-south
direction. It is not possible to see anything above the high hedges, and they
prevent moving in any direction but the path itself. Although a few prints can
be made out on the path, it does not seem to have been used much lately. To the
north and east of here lie several hobbit dwellings with carefully tended
gardens.
name: The Path to Sarn Ford

desc:
This cosy smial has few rooms, comfortable-looking furniture and most
importantly; a well-equipped kitchen that comes with a proper pantry. Several
small windows enlighten the dwelling and allow a view outside where a lightly-
chequered pathway runs through a well-tended garden full of blooming forget-
me-nots and other flowers. To the west stands a perfectly round door painted in
green.
name: Blacktoe's Beautiful Retreat

desc:
Upon the white walls of this small office are a variety of maps. Some look to be
topographical; others seem to show land ownership and easements. A large table
with over a dozen chairs sits at the centre of the room. The table and chairs
fill up so much of the room that there is barely a foot between the chair backs
and the map-covered walls.
name: Deeds, Writs and Records

desc:
This room is cluttered beyond belief. On every wall a thousand things are hung,
and on every shelf and desk another hundred lie. Hanging from the ceiling is a
curious device, boasting three red balls. Behind the counter, some of the more
valuable goods are kept.
name: Pawn Shop

desc:
Rising steeply from the ground, this hill towers above the grasslands to the
east. Occasionally, a thin stream of grey smoke drifts through the air, possibly
from some chimney nearby. The faint path leading up this hill disappears on the
rocks here, but a few large footprints still can be made out at a sporadic
interval. Just below the hill to the east a farmhouse stands in the middle of a
vast pasture.
name: A Hill in the White Downs

desc:
The warm and cozy smial has a desk facing the window with a journal and ink set
upon. The journal has many of Lomek's adventures and journeys written within it,
each page carefully and neatly inked. There is a comfortable bed across from the
desk on the east side of the smial.
name: Lomek's Warm And Cozy Smial

desc:
Pebbles, rocks, and other natural debris cover the narrow valley floor here,
with the hillside looming to great heights in every direction other than the
south. A slight decline can be noticed along the valley floor leading there,
where several patches of yellow and scrawny weeds are struggling to grow.
name: Narrow Valley

desc:
The valley leads to the north and south from here, with a steep hillside that
stands along the west and east. There are rocks and debris scattered along the
valley floor everywhere from the hills decaying over time, with small patches of
weeds growing randomly along the ground.
name: Valley between Steep Hillsides

desc:
A dark hillside stands to both the south and east, where it climbs to a greater
height as it travels north. Patches of rock, pebbles, and other debris that has
decayed from the hills lay scattered along the valley floor leading toward the
west and north. The northern area of the valley seems to have a slight incline.
Patches of yellow grasses and weeds are trying to grow the best they can in the
valley.
name: Winding Valley

desc:
The ground is overgrown with patches of weeds and tall grasses. There are many
areas covered with pebbles and small rocks, and the vegetation appears to
diminish towards the west side of the hill. The pebbles and rocks are dark
coloured and appear to have come from the surrounding hills itself over time.
The steep hillside blocks any travel from the north or the south, and continues
to the east from here.
name: Overgrown Valley

desc:
Since the master of this house is Folko, the Stoor hobbit, this house differs
among other hobbit buildings: it is broader and heavier. In front there is the
entrance to the house, with a door, rounded from the ends, there is a small door
behind you.
name: The Entrance

desc:
You see a large room, with an ancient table and several chairs around it. In the
centre of the dwelling is a huge fireplace and a master's chair near it.
name: The Guests Room

desc:
This chamber looks like a noble's room in king's castle. There is a huge bed,
made from oak, in the right corner. The stairs, leading up, are covered with a
rich carpet. You can also see a huge bookshelf, full of ancient books.
name: Folko's Sleeping Chamber

desc:
This old and stony house is really small. It is mainly a big kitchen, with the
entire northern wall being hidden by the stove. Upon the stove sits a large
kettle emitting a faint whistle. Against the southern wall stands a large table,
covered with big white flour spots. The eastern wall is taken up by a large bed
with a grass mattress. There is nothing against the western wall, except a stair
leading up. The steps are worn out by centuries of heavy feet slouching up and
down them.
name: A Small, Stony House

desc:
This is a green, grass-covered and treeless hill, lost among several others of
its kind. There are no noticeable landmarks. In every direction one can only see
similar hills. Breaking the monotony are a few broken white stones, which rise
out of the eroded ground. A small house stands here, occasionally with smoke
trailing out of the chimney. A few steps lead down to a heavy, stone front door.
name: The Hills of Faraway

desc:
Several identical looking hills lie in all directions. There is not a single
tree breaking the monotony of the grass-covered area. Differentiating the hills
from each other are abstract patterns of sprinkled chalk-stones lying about
them.
name: The Hills of Faraway

desc:
These green and bald hills look similar to all the hills around. Separated by
shallow grass-covered valleys, the hills seem like a vast rolling sea. With the
thin, green grass covering the low-lying hills waving in the wind. Lots of white
stones lie scattered all round, their pattern helping to identify ones
whereabouts.
name: The Hills of Faraway

desc:
Bald hills, covered by thin, green grass, characterise this area. In every
direction, identical looking hills can be seen. If not for the white chalky
stones littering a few of the hills, it would be hard to differentiate them from
each other. Sloping, grass-covered valleys lie between the rolling hills.
name: The Hills of Faraway

desc:
This underground room, carved out of the chalky stone of the downs, is sealed
with a heavy oaken door, reinforced with black iron. It is used by the
Shire-folk to restrain evildoers. There are very few Shire-folk who are
restrained so it's mainly used for outlanders and as a storeroom in harvest-time
by the practical-minded hobbits. The mingled smells of apple, wine, pipe-weed,
decayed peas, and old cheese are rather overpowering. None of these foodstuffs
are being kept here now, though. Some straw mats have been spread on the floor.
During the day, light filters dimly through a heavy grille in the door.
name: The Lockholes
